data.title: CarMax: Early Signs of Volume Improvement but Limited Visibility on Durable Earnings Recovery Justify Hold Rating
data.description:
Needham analyst Chris Pierce has reiterated their neutral stance on KMX stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Chris Pierce has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to CarMax’s current operating backdrop and recovery outlook. He sees some early signs that sales volumes may begin to improve, supported by normal seasonal trends and a slightly more assertive cost-cutting plan, which could help near‑term results and lead to upward revisions in estimates.

However, Pierce believes the visibility into a durable earnings rebound is still limited, as CarMax must demonstrate it can expand unit volumes without eroding profitability. He also highlights that competitive pressure, particularly from Carvana, remains a major wildcard, leaving the shares appearing inexpensive on longer‑term valuation metrics but still carrying considerable risk around the trajectory and timing of earnings recovery.

In another report released today, William Blair also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $49.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Intercos: Strengthening Makeup Momentum and Margin Expansion Support Continued Buy Rating
data.description:
In a report released today, David Hayes from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on Intercos S.p.A., with a price target of €15.30.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Hayes has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including an improving outlook for the makeup segment where Intercos is particularly well positioned thanks to its strong R&D capabilities. While he anticipates a relatively weak first quarter, he expects momentum to build as a robust order pipeline translates into stronger performance through 2026.

He forecasts that makeup will be the primary driver of organic sales growth next year, with his top-line expectations slightly above consensus and a gradual enhancement in EBITDA margins as targeted investments pay off. In addition, his unchanged target price implies a meaningful price-to-earnings multiple expansion for Intercos, supporting a positive risk‑reward profile and justifying the continued Buy recommendation.

Hayes covers the Consumer Defensive sector, focusing on stocks such as Unilever, Nestlé SA, and Puig Brands, S.A.. According to TipRanks, Hayes has an average return of 3.3% and a 59.34% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 2, UBS also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a €16.10 price target.
